message:
feat(snapshot_engine): tick 153 capture_from_provider cx-first sibling + scheduler body routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Fourth post-storage tick. snapshot_engine.rs had 6 `_with_cx`
methods; naive scan flagged 5 "missing" siblings. Four of those
are false positives — `spawn_blocking_db` and
`spawn_blocking_db_best_effort` are generic helpers, `scheduler_body`
already takes cx by name (parameter, not suffix), and
`scheduler_body_legacy` is the non-asupersync-only path. The one
true target is `capture_from_provider`.

New sibling:
- capture_from_provider_with_cx — routes both `self.capture` and
  `self.cleanup` through their cx-first siblings
  (`capture_with_cx`, `cleanup_with_cx`) so every write seam inside
  the scheduler's work loop honours caller cancellation. Tracing
  uses the same messages suffixed with "(cx path)" for grep
  distinction. Shape-preserving: the pane provider is still
  awaited directly — it's a caller-supplied future that doesn't
  inherently know about cx; callers who want cx threading inside
  the provider can capture one in their closure.

Call-site migrations inside `scheduler_body` (cx-aware scheduler
body at L925) — 4 call sites, all on the work loop:
- Periodic mode periodic capture (was L907)
- Intelligent mode startup capture (was L925)
- Intelligent mode trigger-driven capture (was L991)
- Intelligent mode periodic-fallback capture (was L1008; multi-line
  call, cx inserted as first arg).

`scheduler_body_legacy` (non-asupersync path) is untouched — 4
remaining legacy call sites there still route through the ambient
non-cx helpers.

Verified via fork bypass: 92/92 snapshot_engine:: tests pass,
including the existing `run_periodic_with_cx_mid_flight_cancel_exits_quickly`
regression gate which now transitively proves the cx-first capture
path is interruptible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
